Description

Overview:

Red Rover is seeking an organized, proactive, and eager-to-learn budding marketer or early-career marketing professional to support the execution, coordination, and day-to-day operations of our growing marketing organization as a Marketing Associate.

The Marketing Associate plays an important role in helping ongoing campaigns, programs, and initiatives stay organized, execute successfully, and remain aligned across marketing and cross-functional teams.

This role collaborates across teams, contributing to a wide variety of marketing initiatives and projects. Working alongside experienced marketing leaders, the Marketing Associate will build a broad foundation across campaign marketing, product marketing, content strategy, customer marketing, field marketing and events, sales enablement, and go-to-market execution while helping the team operate more efficiently as Red Rover continues to grow.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, collaborative, and detail-oriented, and enjoys variety, continuous learning, and fast-moving projects. They're eager to gain hands-on experience across all aspects of modern B2B SaaS marketing.

What Youâll Do

Marketing campaign & program support

  • Support the planning, coordination, and execution of integrated marketing campaigns and strategic initiatives across multiple marketing programs
  • Assist with email campaign preparation and scheduling, webinar coordination and preparation, campaign timelines, promotional communications, marketing calendar coordination, campaign asset coordination, website & CRM management, and cross-functional coordination
  • Help ensure campaigns remain organized, on schedule, and aligned across marketing and sales teams

Cross-functional coordination & team support

  • Help coordinate projects, communication, and day-to-day marketing initiatives across sales, SDR, and marketing teams
  • Support activities including project timeline management, meeting preparation, action item tracking, workflow coordination, internal communications, marketing request management, project documentation, team follow-up

Event & field marketing support

  • Support the planning and execution of webinars, conferences, customer events, regional marketing initiatives, and field marketing programs alongside marketing team
  • Assist with webinar event logistics, registration management, presentation and speaker coordination, promotional campaigns and communications, sales coordination, post-event follow-ups, etc.
  • Assist with strategic planning and onsite execution logistics for tradeshows, conferences, and regional events
  • Help contribute to memorable customer and prospect experiences before, during, and after each event

Content & marketing asset coordination

  • Support the marketing organization in publishing and maintaining marketing content library and sales resources across multiple channels
  • Assist with blog publishing, marketing asset organization, webinar materials, sales collateral materials, digital asset management, social media scheduling, website content coordination, and content library management

Marketing operations & team organization

  • Help maintain the operational foundation that keeps the marketing organization running efficiently
  • Support activities including project tracking, campaign organization, campaign performance reporting support, workflow management, budget tracking assistance, tradeshow and events calendar management, etc.
  • Assist in marketing database updates and campaign and program builds alongside marketing operations team

Professional growth & continuous learning

  • Take an active role in developing marketing knowledge and expanding level of responsibilities over time
  • Demonstrate curiosity, initiative, and a willingness to continuously improve and learn while eager to build experience and deepen knowledge across demand generation, campaign marketing, product marketing, brand marketing, content strategy, customer marketing and advocacy, field marketing and events, marketing operations, go-to-market strategy, and AI and traditional or emerging marketing technologies

Requirements

Qualifications:

  • 0-3 years of experience in marketing, communications, business, customer-facing roles, events, or related fields
  • Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field preferred
  • Strong organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail, proven project management skills preferred
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Positive attitude with a willingness to learn and take initiative
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
  • Strong follow-through and reliability
  • Comfortable working cross-functionally with multiple teams
  • Familiarity with Microsoft Office and SharePoint/OneDrive
  • Experience with Canva, HubSpot, or similar marketing platforms and tools is a plus
  • Interest in SaaS, education technology, or B2B marketing environments preferred
  • Ability to travel 15-20% to attend department summits, company events, and regional/sales events

Bonus Skills

  • Experience supporting marketing campaigns, events, or social media initiatives
  • Familiarity with email marketing platforms or marketing automation tools
  • Basic graphic design experience using Canva or Adobe Express
  • Experience with project management platforms such as Asana, Monday.com, or Airtable
  • Experience writing or editing marketing content
  • Interest in AI tools that improve marketing productivity and execution
  • Exposure to CRM platforms such as HubSpot or Salesforce
  • Experience working with K-12 education or other mission-driven organizations

Umpisa Inc. is a progressive technology services company that partners with select industries, clients and people to work on pioneering and industry-changing solutions via digital transformation, modern software development and venture building.

Essential Skills:

  • Strong communication skills
  • Strong problem solving and analytical skills
  • Excellent problem-solving ability

Job Summary

We are looking for a dedicated and customer-focused Help Desk / IT Support Specialist to provide first-level technical support to internal users. The ideal candidate has 2-5 years of IT support experience, strong troubleshooting skills, and a passion for delivering excellent technical assistance while ensuring a positive user experience.

Requirements

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide first-line technical support for hardware, software, and network-related issues
  • Quickly respond to support requests via ticketing system, email, chat, or phone
  • Troubleshoot and resolve common IT issues (PCs, laptops, printers, applications, connectivity, etc.)
  • Escalate complex issues to appropriate teams when necessary
  • Document issues, resolutions, and procedures clearly and accurately
  • Assist with user account setup, access management, and basic system administration

  • Strong problem-solving and communication skills

Nice to Have

  • Experience with ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, ServiceNow, Zendesk, etc.)

  • IT-related certification is a plus but not required

Work Setup & Schedule

  • Remote set-up
  • Shift schedule: Mid-Shift from 3:00PM onwards
  • Shift flexibility may be considered in the future based on performance and team needs

Company Description

Cooinda Lodge Kakadu is a unique resort nestled in the heart of Kakadu National Park, Northern Territory, Australia. Under a Accor's Hotel Management Agreement in partnership with the Djigardaba Enterprise Aboriginal Corporation, it offers guests a gateway to explore the UNESCO World Heritage-listed park. The lodge provides a variety of accommodations, including lodge rooms, eco-tents, and camping sites, along with amenities such as two swimming pools, restaurants, bars, a general store, and access to Yellow Water Cruises and the Warradjan Cultural Centre.

Cooinda Lodge is proudly committed to Indigenous employment, with 25% of our team Bining, working on country. As part of our ongoing support into Indigenous employment and development, we collaborate with the Indigenous Land and Sea Corporation through the Real Jobs Program.

We are an equal opportunity employer, committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and identities.

You must have the legal right to work in Australia to be considered for this role.

Job Description

  • Performing daily checks and minor repairs (e.g. fixing leaking taps, replacing lightbulbs, clearing pathways, inspect and clean air conditioning filters, clean and maintain pool filters, etc.).
  • Maintaining guest facilities and lodge equipment (e.g. air-conditioning filter cleaning, pool upkeep,etc.).
  • Supporting scheduled preventive maintenance (e.g. servicing generators, inspecting roofing and water heaters, conduct pest and termite inspection, etc.).

Qualifications

  • 1â2 yearsâ experience in a similar role, with understanding of basic plumbing, electrical, HVAC, and general repair work.
  • Reliable, committed, and adaptable to shift work; ability to multitask and manage time effectively in a dynamic hospitality environment.
  • Good interpersonal skills, integrity, and a strong commitment to guest satisfaction, safety, and Accorâs Heartist values.

Additional Information

Cooinda Lodge Kakadu, offers a unique opportunity to work in one of Australia's most iconic locations, surrounded by natural beauty and rich cultural heritage. We provide staff accommodation and meals on-site, ensuring a comfortable and supportive work environment. Join our team and be part of an unforgettable experience in the heart of Kakadu National Park.

We Offer

  • Subsidised Housing and meals provided
  • Unique work environment surrounded by natural and cultural heritage.

Itâs a lofty goal; we know. But we make it happen with the best team in behavioral health. 

 

Thank you for taking the time to explore a career with us. As the fastest growing behavioral health practice group in the country, now is the perfect time to join our clinical team!

 


We are actively looking to hire a talented remote therapist (LPC, LCSW or LMFT) to support our growing Houston, TX offices, who is passionate about patient care and committed to clinical excellence.

\n


Is this you?
  • Wanting to deliver high quality behavioral healthcare.

What we offer Therapists:

Customer Care & Administrative Coordinator (Remote)

TruBlue of Delmarva is seeking an organized, energetic, and customer-focused Customer Care & Administrative Coordinator to join our growing team. This position plays a vital role in ensuring every customer receives exceptional service from their first phone call through project completion.

We're looking for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys helping people, and takes pride in staying organized. If you're a self-starter with excellent communication skills and a passion for providing outstanding customer service, we'd love to hear from you.

Responsibilities

Customer Service
- Answer incoming phone calls and respond to customer inquiries professionally and promptly.
- Respond quickly to web leads from Google, Thumbtack, and other marketing sources.
- Schedule estimates and service appointments using Housecall Pro.
- Confirm upcoming appointments and communicate schedule changes with customers.
- Conduct customer satisfaction follow-up calls after completed projects.
- Request Google reviews and maintain positive customer relationships.

Sales & Office Support
- Follow up on outstanding estimates and assist in converting leads into booked jobs.
- Maintain accurate customer records and job notes within Housecall Pro.
- Assist with invoicing, collecting deposits, and tracking customer communications.
- Coordinate calendars for the owner and project managers.
- Support daily office operations and administrative tasks.

Marketing Support
- Coordinate social media content and before-and-after project photos.
- Assist with email marketing campaigns and customer newsletters.
- Help maintain our online reputation by monitoring reviews and customer feedback.

General Administration
- Organize digital files and maintain accurate records.
- Assist with purchasing office supplies and coordinating vendors when needed.
- Continuously improve administrative processes and workflows.
- Perform other administrative and customer service duties as assigned.

Qualifications

- High school diploma or equivalent required.
- Previous experience in customer service, scheduling, administrative support, dispatching, or office management preferred.
- Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
- Strong organizational and time management abilities.
- Comfortable multitasking in a fast-paced environment.
- Friendly, positive, and professional demeanor.
- Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
- Proficiency with Microsoft Office and Google Workspace.
- Experience with CRM or scheduling software (Housecall Pro experience is a plus but not required).

Preferred Experience

Experience in one or more of the following industries is highly desirable:
- Home services
- Construction
- Property management
- HVAC
- Plumbing
- Electrical
- Restoration
- Insurance or real estate offices

What Success Looks Like

The successful candidate will:
- Answer customer inquiries promptly and professionally.
- Respond to new leads within minutes whenever possible.
- Help keep our schedule organized and efficient.
- Deliver exceptional customer experiences.
- Maintain accurate records and follow-through on every customer interaction.
- Contribute to the continued growth of our company.

About TruBlue of Delmarva

TruBlue of Delmarva is a locally owned, veteran-owned home services company providing handyman, painting, remodeling, and home maintenance services throughout the Delmarva Peninsula. We are committed to delivering exceptional craftsmanship, outstanding customer service, and honest communication on every project.

We believe our employees are our greatest asset. We foster a supportive team environment where initiative, professionalism, and customer satisfaction are recognized and rewarded.

Benefits

- Competitive hourly pay
- Flexible schedule
- Paid training
- Paid time off (eligible employees)
- Opportunities for advancement as the company grows
- Supportive team environment
- Remote position
- Performance-based bonuses and incentives

If you're looking for an opportunity to make a meaningful impact with a growing company that values integrity, teamwork, and exceptional customer service, we'd love to hear from you.

RYZ Labs is looking for a Pre-Press Retoucher to join one of our clientâs teams, from the sports merchandising industry. The ideal candidate will translate card designs into beautiful, error-free, and quality-checked forms that are ready for the press. This professional will support imaging, color correction, and retouching of card images/designs in line with the Pre-Press teamâs commitment to excellence.

  • Ensure all images meet color accuracy and consistency requirements

RYZ Labs is a startup studio built in 2021 by two lifelong entrepreneurs. The founders of RYZ have worked at some of the world's largest tech companies and some of the most iconic consumer brands. What brought them together is the passion for the early phases of company creation and the idea of attracting the brightest talents in order to build industry-defining companies in a post-pandemic world.

Our teams are remote and distributed throughout the US and Latam. They use the latest cutting-edge technologies in cloud computing to create applications that are scalable and resilient. We aim to provide diverse product solutions for different industries, planning to build a large number of startups in the upcoming years.

At RYZ, you will find yourself working with autonomy and efficiency, owning every step of your development. We provide an environment of opportunities, learning, growth, expansion and challenging projects. You will deepen your experience while sharing and learning from a team of great professionals and specialists.

  • Customer First Mentality - every decision we make should be made through the lens of the customer
  • Bias for Action - urgency is critical, expect that the timeline to get something done is accelerated
  • Ownership - step up if you see an opportunity to help, even if not your core responsibility
  • Humility and Respect - be willing to learn, be vulnerable, and treat everyone that interacts with RYZ with respect
  • Frugality - being frugal and cost-conscious helps us do more with less
  • Deliver Impact - get things done in the most efficient way
  • Raise our Standards - always be looking to improve our processes, our team, our expectations. Status quo is not good enough and never should be

Location: Remote (Washington State preferred)

6 month contract to hire

Pay: $30-32/hr


Required Skills & Experience


Qualifications:

- 3+ years of experience in Human Resources, Talent Acquisition, or a combined HR/Recruiting role.

- Experience managing payroll, benefits administration, and employee lifecycle processes.

- Proven full-cycle recruiting experience.

- Strong knowledge of employment laws and HR best practices.

- Experience with HRIS, payroll, and applicant tracking systems.

- Excellent communication, organizational, and relationship-building skills.

Job Description


Our client is seeking an HR Generalist & Recruiter to oversee all aspects of people operations and recruiting. This role is a 50/50 blend of Human Resources and Talent Acquisition, supporting a remote workforce while helping attract, hire, and retain top talent across legal, immigration, and business operations functions. The ideal candidate is a hands-on HR professional who is equally comfortable managing payroll and benefits as they are sourcing and recruiting candidates. Human Resources

- Administer employee benefits programs, enrollments, and employee support.

- Manage workforce planning, employee onboarding, and offboarding activities.

- Maintain compliance with federal, state, and local employment laws.

- Support employee relations, performance management, and policy administration.

- Maintain HRIS and employee documentation.

- Partner with leadership on workforce management and organizational planning.


Talent Acquisition

- Lead full-cycle recruiting for legal, immigration, paralegal, attorney, case management, and corporate support positions.

- Partner with hiring managers to define hiring needs and recruitment strategies.

- Source, screen, interview, and coordinate candidates throughout the hiring process.

- Manage job postings, applicant tracking systems, and recruiting metrics. - Build talent pipelines for future hiring needs.

- Create a positive candidate experience and strengthen the employer brand.


Benefits packages for this role will start on the 1st day of employment and include medical, dental, and vision insurance, as well as HSA, FSA, and DCFSA account options, and 401k retirement account access with employer matching. Employees in this role are also entitled to paid sick leave and/or other paid time off as provided by applicable law

The verdict

Rippling is one of the strongest products you can buy if you run HR, IT, and finance as connected systems, and the review data backs that up: 4.8 across more than 14,000 G2 reviews and 4.9 on Capterra. The onboarding automation that provisions payroll, benefits, IT access, and a device in one move is the standout, and it earns its keep. Go in clear-eyed about two things: the modular, quote-based pricing stacks faster than people expect, and the first-week learning curve is real.

Key features

One employee record that drives everything

Hire someone once and the record propagates everywhere: payroll, benefits, device provisioning, app access, and offboarding all read from it, so a department change updates downstream automatically.

Automated payroll and tax compliance

Rippling calculates payroll taxes and files them with the correct federal, state, and local agencies, supports multi-entity and global payroll for employees and contractors, and stores electronic W-2s and 1099s.

One-click onboarding and offboarding

A single flow sends offer letters, enrolls payroll and benefits, ships a device, and grants app logins; offboarding runs it in reverse, revoking access and triggering device returns.

Benefits administration with payroll sync

Over 500 EDI and API carrier integrations, integrated HSA/FSA/commuter accounts with automatic payroll-deduction sync, COBRA and ACA automation, and bring-your-own-broker support alongside Rippling's PEO and brokerage.

600+ out-of-the-box integrations

Connects QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, Guideline for 401(k), Checkr for background checks, plus identity and productivity tools, with real user provisioning, attribute mapping, and cross-app workflow automation.

Modular HR, IT, and Finance products

Buy the core Rippling Platform, then add only the products you need across HR (Payroll, Benefits, Learning, Performance), IT (Identity, Device, Inventory), and Finance (Corporate Cards, Expenses, Bill Pay).

What it is

Rippling is an HR platform that doesnât stop at HR. The core is a single employee record: hire someone once, and that record drives payroll, benefits, device provisioning, app access, and offboarding. Where most HR software treats the employee database as a system of record, Rippling treats it as a trigger. Change a personâs department and Rippling updates it everywhere downstream. Onboard a new hire and it enrolls them in payroll, sets up their benefits, ships a laptop, and grants the right app logins from the same flow.

The product is sold in modules. Thereâs a core, required Rippling Platform, and then you buy what you need on top of it: HR products (Payroll, Benefits, Learning, Performance, Recruiting, Time & Attendance), IT products (Identity & Access, Device, Inventory), and Finance products (Corporate Cards, Expenses, Bill Pay). The pitch, and itâs a real one, is that all three sit on the same data, so the IT and finance sides arenât bolted-on afterthoughts.

Who itâs for

This is built for the company that has outgrown a point HR tool but is also tired of running payroll in one place, device management in another, and expense reports in a third. If youâre hiring across multiple states or countries, Ripplingâs strongest. It runs multi-entity and global payroll, stores W-2s and 1099s, and handles contractors through dedicated products. Reviewers who pay people internationally describe currency conversion and cross-border pay as something Rippling âjust takes care of.â

Itâs a worse fit for a simple operation. If you have fifteen domestic employees, one benefits plan, and no real IT footprint, the all-in-one model is overkill, and reviewers say exactly that, warning that simpler teams risk overbuying. Youâd be paying for breadth you wonât use and absorbing a configuration burden you donât need. Rippling rewards companies that genuinely run HR, IT, and finance as connected systems. If those live in separate departments that never talk, you lose the main reason to buy it.

Why it stands out

Onboarding and offboarding is the standout, and itâs not close. Capterra logs 97% positive mentions on onboarding, with reviewers describing clear step-by-step guidance to add employees, configure payroll, and connect benefits. business.com calls it a major strength. The thing people keep describing is âone-click onboardingâ that provisions payroll, IT access, and a device in a single action, and the same automation runs in reverse at offboarding, revoking access and triggering device returns. The January 2026 release added advanced Microsoft 365 offboarding actions, which is exactly the kind of detail that matters when someone leaves.

The unified platform is the second genuine win. Capterra users rate the all-in-one dashboard 98% positive, and reviewers repeatedly call out Ripplingâs UI as the best theyâve used in an HRMS. Collapsing payroll, benefits, and people data into one synced record removes the manual re-entry and the reconciliation errors that plague stitched-together stacks.

Integration breadth is the third. Rippling ships 600+ (the FAQ says 650+) out-of-the-box integrations: QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, Guideline for 401(k), Checkr for background checks, plus the usual identity and productivity tools, with real provisioning and attribute mapping, not just data export. Reviewers call this exceptional. The one caveat worth flagging: Expert Market notes Rippling doesnât natively integrate with third-party HR systems, so the strength is in app provisioning and internal cross-module sync rather than swapping out parts of the HR stack.

Pricing in plain language

Rippling starts around $8 per employee per month, but that number tells you very little. The real model is modular and quote-based: thereâs no full price list on the site, and some products carry a monthly base fee on top of the per-employee rate. You buy the core platform, then add each product separately. Thatâs the flexibility selling point and the budgeting headache in one.

Hereâs where the wall is: the modules stack. Independent reviewers are consistent on this, the per-employee base is reasonable, but as you add Benefits, Device Management, Expenses, and the rest, the total climbs in ways that arenât obvious upfront. The most common complaint across G2, Capterra, and third-party reviews isnât that Rippling is bad value, itâs that the total cost is hard to see coming. Benefits administration is a particular gotcha: itâs a separate paid module, which is why business.com calls benefits a ânotable weaknessâ on cost grounds, though those fees can be waived if Rippling is your broker or PEO.

Get a real quote before you fall in love with the demo, and price the modules youâll actually turn on, not the ones that look nice in the sales deck.

Limitations

The learning curve is real. The same feature density that makes Rippling powerful makes it overwhelming on day one. G2 reviewers describe it as a lot to absorb, especially for new admins. This is the cost of the all-in-one model, and itâs worth budgeting setup time for.

Stability is the more concerning one. Capterra reviewers flag platform bugs and slow loads (48% negative) and auth or session timeouts (46% negative), and the mobile app comes up as occasionally buggy and missing features the desktop version has. More seriously, payroll, the thing you most need to be boring and correct, has edge cases. Capterra reviewers report incorrect overtime calculations that forced them to claw back overpayments, and Thrivea notes tax edge-case issues. The payroll engine is rated highly overall, with a 100% error-free filing guarantee cited by business.com, but the exceptions are the kind you remember.

Reporting is the quieter gap. 43% of Capterra mentions on reporting are negative, centered on a lack of customization. If you need to slice your people data in non-standard ways, expect to fight the report builder. Benefits enrollment is the last one worth naming: Thrivea reports the workflows can feel clunky or incomplete, which is part of why benefits sentiment runs mixed even though the payroll-to-deduction sync is well liked.

The bottom line

If youâre a growing company running HR, IT, and finance as connected systems, especially across states or borders, Rippling is one of the strongest products you can buy, and the review data backs that up. The onboarding automation alone earns its keep, and the unified record genuinely removes work. Buy it for the integration, not just the HR.

But go in clear-eyed. Get a real quote and add up every module youâll switch on, because the stacking is where people get surprised. Budget setup time for the learning curve. And if youâre a small, single-country team with simple benefits and no IT footprint, this is more platform than you need, youâll pay for breadth you wonât touch. Rippling rewards complexity. Bring it the right kind, and itâs excellent.

What people are saying online

Rippling is one of the most highly rated HR platforms I track, 4.8 across more than 14,000 G2 reviews and 4.9 on Capterra. Users love the unified dashboard and the way onboarding fires off payroll, benefits, and IT setup in one move. The platform is most positive for growing companies that genuinely run HR, IT, and finance together and have the bandwidth to configure it. The recurring frustrations are the same ones every time: a steep first-week learning curve, occasional bugs and slow loads, and a modular price tag that stacks up faster than people expect.

About Bizee

Bizee has helped nearly two million entrepreneurs start and run their businesses. Now we are building what comes next: an AI-powered Entrepreneur Operating System that helps founders launch, operate, stay compliant, and grow with less friction and more confidence.

We are profitable, founder-led, and operating at a clear inflection point. The next chapter requires a stronger operating system for how we hire, lead, communicate, develop talent, and scale execution across the company.

The Opportunity

We are looking for a Head of People & Organizational Effectiveness to serve as a strategic partner to the President and executive team.

This is not a maintenance HR role. It is a builder-operator role for someone who can create clarity, raise the talent bar, strengthen the leadership layer, and build the people systems that help Bizee scale without unnecessary complexity or organizational drag.

As Bizee grows and transforms, one of the most important responsibilities of this role will be building and protecting a high talent bar. We are not simply adding headcount; we are intentionally increasing talent density across the company. This leader will help ensure we hire high-caliber builders, operators, and leaders who raise the standard, strengthen execution, and fit the culture we are building.

You will help design how Bizee hires, onboards, develops, manages, communicates, and performs as we move into our next stage of growth.

What You Will Own

  • Partner with the President and executive team on org design, leadership effectiveness, talent quality, succession, performance, and cross-functional friction
  • Design and protect Bizee's talent bar. Build the hiring standards, role scorecards, interview calibration, decision rules, and leadership alignment required to consistently identify high-caliber talent as the company scales. Ensure we do not compromise on quality-of-hire in the name of speed
  • Design a scalable hiring system, including workforce planning, role definition, scorecards, interview loops, calibration, decision frameworks, and candidate experience
  • Build Bizee's People Operating System, including the rhythms, mechanisms, and rituals that drive alignment, accountability, and performance
  • Strengthen the manager layer through practical enablement, feedback systems, performance routines, and leadership expectations
  • Design and own Bizee's performance cycle: goal setting, ratings, calibration, and linkage to comp and promotion decisions
  • Advise on role clarity, leveling, compensation philosophy, incentive design, spans, layers, and capability planning
  • Build onboarding, engagement, and internal communication mechanisms that help employees ramp faster and stay connected to the mission
  • Lead change management as Bizee modernizes its platform, operating model, AI-enabled workflows, and distributed teams
  • Plan and execute the eventual transition away from full co-employment (PEO) arrangements in multiple countries globally, including building the internal HRIS, payroll, benefits and compliance infrastructure needed to stand up direct employment, sequencing the migration to minimize risk and disruption, and managing the associated legal, tax, and employee communication considerations.
  • Own multi-state wage and hour compliance and workforce administration for a distributed employee population spanning nearly all 50 U.S. states, encompassing both exempt and non-exempt staff
  • Lead and integrate teams across multiple countries internationally, ensuring a single coherent Bizee culture and set of operating standards translate effectively across geographies, management structures, and local labor norms

Requirements

What You Will Bring

  • 10+ years of progressive experience in People, Talent, HRBP, Organizational Effectiveness, or People Operations leadership
  • Experience building People systems from the ground up, ideally in a high-growth, profitable, founder-led, PE-backed, SaaS, platform, marketplace, or tech-enabled services environment
  • Strong executive judgment and the ability to operate as a true strategic partner to a President and executive team
  • Proven experience with hiring systems, talent calibration, performance management, manager enablement, org design, leveling, compensation frameworks, and People Ops infrastructure
  • A strong point of view on talent density, quality-of-hire, leadership standards, and what it takes to build a high-performance company
  • Strong operator instincts: structured, practical, clear, and biased toward action
  • High EQ and high backbone: able to coach leaders, challenge assumptions, handle sensitive issues, and create clarity without adding drama or bureaucracy
  • Strategic and hands-on: able to advise the executive team in the morning and fix an onboarding workflow in the afternoon
  • Business-first orientation: understands revenue, margin, productivity, execution, and the tradeoffs of scaling a profitable company
  • Low-drama, high-signal communication style: brings facts, judgment, candor, and calm to complex situations
  • Culture carrier: protects what makes Bizee special while raising the performance bar
  • Comfort operating in ambiguity and building while the business is moving fast
  • Strong written communication skills and the ability to turn messy organizational issues into simple plans and operating mechanisms
  • Demonstrated experience managing a multi-state U.S. workforce (ideally 40+ states) that includes both exempt and non-exempt employees, with working knowledge of state-by-state wage and hour variation and how to operationalize compliance without adding unnecessary drag
  • Experience leading or partnering with teams in multiple countries, with a track record of integrating culture, standards, and management practices across borders rather than running parallel, disconnected functions
  • Direct experience transitioning a workforce out of a co-employment/PEO model, including standing up in-country or in-state direct employment infrastructure (payroll, benefits, statutory compliance) and managing the change process for affected employees and managers

What Success Looks Like

  • In the first year, Bizee has a stronger People Operating System that improves alignment, ownership, speed, and accountability across the company
  • Hiring is more structured, faster, and higher-signal. Leaders know what great looks like by role and level, hiring decisions are more disciplined, and the company is increasing talent density as it scales
  • Managers are better equipped to set direction, coach performance, handle conflict, and create clarity. Employees have stronger expectations, better communication, and a clearer understanding of how their work connects to the company's mission

The Head of People & Organizational Effectiveness becomes a trusted advisor to the President, executive team, and leadership layer, helping Bizee scale with discipline, clarity, high standards, and good judgment.

  • Hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure (GCP preferred), containers and Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.
  • Comfort with both SQL and NoSQL data stores (MongoDB, SingleStore/MySQL/Postgres) and an eye for data consistency in money-critical flows.
  • Deep understanding of reliability patterns â idempotency, retries, reconciliation, graceful degradation â and why they matter when handling real money.

description:
Company Description


Here's the problem we're obsessed with: when you want to do something in your free time, you open TikTok or you ask ChatGPT and both send you to the exact same place as everyone else. The same viral pasta spot, the same rooftop, the same top-10 list. The algorithm flattens a whole city into five places and funnels everyone into them.


But there's no single Milan! There are as many as there are pairs of eyes looking at it, and yours looks nothing like the top-10 everyone else gets fed. Hic finds your version of the city and lets you live it in one tap. We're live in Milan, Florence, Berlin, London, Madrid, and Zurich.


Our content fights the same fight: not "5 things to do in Milan" (that's the enemy), but the specific, the hidden, the strange. Restaurants worth crossing town for, museums that aren't boring, bars, corners, and the stories nobody told you. The stuff that makes someone feel like the city was built for them. That's what you'd be making.




Role Description


We don't want to hand you a template. We want someone to own our TikTok voice and run with it: your ideas, your style, your hooks. You'll have the creative freedom to make people fall in love with Hic, however you see it, working hand in hand with the founder. You'd make around 4 short videos a week, with room to grow: shoot Milan on the ground, and edit footage for the other cities. This is paid freelance, project-based to start, part-time. A young app whose whole voice you get to shape. The only rule: it's about real places people can actually go. What it's not: a corporate content job or an unpaid "exposure" gig.




Qualifications


  • You are original: (you bring ideas, you've got taste and a point of view).
  • You live on TikTok and it shows (you get hooks and retention in your bones).
  • You have an account that actually posts.
  • Fast in CapCut (or whatever gets it done).
  • Based in Milan, and you can go shoot on location.
  • You own the creative, but you're happy to shape strategy together with the team.




If you are interested DM us your TikTok account or one you manage!

Who We Are

iPiD empowers financial institutions, payment service providers, and enterprises with a single global API for Know Your Payee (KYP) verificationâreducing fraud, reducing costs, staying compliant, and streamlining cross-border payments, globally.

We are a fast-growing fintech startup, founded by a global team of industry veterans from iconic organizations like SWIFT and Thomson Reuters. Supported by leading investors such as Monk's Hill Ventures, Jungle Ventures and Saison Capital, and guided by an advisory team featuring C-suite leaders from Microsoft, Commonwealth Bank, and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. Our team combines global expertise with local insights; we are present across key citiesâincluding Singapore, Hanoi, Kuala Lumpur, Mumbai, Amsterdam, Brussels, Paris, London, New York, and Dubai.

Role Overview

As Sales Director you will play a key role in driving business growth by identifying and securing new opportunities across the region. This is a high-impact commercial role for someone who thrives on building strategic relationships, opening new markets, and delivering revenue growth within the cross-border payments industry. Working closely with cross-functional teams, you will help shape our commercial strategy and expand our presence across Europe.

Core Responsibilities

  • Develop and implement commercial strategies according to company goals and objectives aiming to accelerate growth across Europe
  • Conduct market research and analysis to create detailed business plans on commercial opportunities (expansion, business development etc.)
  • Understand the requirements of existing customers to ensure their needs are being met
  • Act to acquire new customers and manage client relationships (new and existing)
  • Collaborate with and coordinate diverse teams (product management, marketing, customer success etc.)
  • Build and maintain profitable partnerships with key stakeholders
  • Monitor performance of commercial activities using key metrics and prepare reports for senior management
  • Assist in setting financial targets and budget development and monitoring

Requirements

  • Strong academic background in engineering, business, or comparable experience
  • Result-oriented with at least 5 years of experience and a track record in the payment industry, preferably cross-border payments
  • Proven experience as commercial director or other relevant role in the Europe
  • Proven experience in sales and/or marketing and managing relationships with key clients
  • In-depth understanding of market research methods and analysis
  • Solid knowledge of performance reporting and financial/budgeting processes
  • Commercial awareness partnered with a strategic mindset
  • Excellent organizational and leadership skills
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English and at least two major European languages (e.g., French, German, Spanish, or Italian).
  • Ability to communicate clearly and confidently with technical and business audiences
  • Candidates may be based anywhere in Europe and must have valid work authorization. Visa sponsorship is not available.

Benefits

What We Offer

  • Meaningful Impact - Play a key role in shaping the future of trusted cross-border payments and fraud prevention, helping solve critical challenges for financial institutions and businesses worldwide
  • Learn from experienced industry leaders - Join a team with deep expertise across payments, fintech and technology, and gain exposure to global customers and markets
  • Ownership & Growth - Join a fast-growing global fintech where your contributions are visible and valued, with the opportunity to participate in our Employee Stock Option Plan (ESOP) and share in our long-term success

Job Summary

Under general supervision, the Cashier performs clerical work in collecting payments from customers.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities

  • Accepts mail payments and payments dropped in the overnight depository, and records amount paid on cash register
  • Clears cash register at the end of the day; totals stubs and receipts; counts cash and checks and compares total on cash register with currency in register to verify balances

  • Some knowledge of the method and equipment used in data entry and computer utilization
  • Ability to plan work and carry through to completion without close supervision

  • Skill in the operation of adding machines and other mechanical office aids

Education & Experience

position: Applied AI Product Strategy & Revenue Lead
description:
Applied AI Product Strategy & Revenue Lead

Own Your Intelligence

Prime Intellect is building the open superintelligence stack: the infrastructure frontier AI labs build internally, made available to every ambitious AI team.

Our platform, Lab, unifies compute, environments, evaluations, secure sandboxes, high-performance training, and deployment into one full-stack system for post-training at frontier scale - from SFT and RL to tool use, agent workflows, and continuously improving production models. We are building open frontier AI: open-source models trained end to end for long-horizon tasks like autonomous research, and the full-stack platform our own research team uses to build them. The next generation of AI companies, enterprises, and research teams do not just need more GPUs. They need the ability to turn their own workflows, tools, data, and feedback loops into superintelligence they own.

Prime Intellect has raised $150M in total funding from Founders Fund, Radical Ventures, NVIDIA, and exceptional AI, infrastructure, and enterprise operators â including Andrej Karpathy, Dwarkesh Patel, and leaders and founders from Ramp, Perplexity, Harvey, Mercor, Zapier, Datadog, Cognition, OpenAI, Thinking Machines, Together AI, SemiAnalysis, LangChain, Browserbase, Cloudflare, Sierra, Databricks, Airbnb, OpenRouter, Standard Intelligence, Fleet, Core Auto, and more. We are looking for people who want to build at the intersection of frontier research, real infrastructure, and go-to-market for a category that does not fully exist yet.


The Role

This is not a traditional sales role. It is not a traditional product role. It is not a traditional solutions engineering role.

You will help define how Prime Intellect turns frontier post-training infrastructure into a product customers can understand, buy, deploy, and expand.

Today, the hardest part of the business is not selling raw compute. It is refining the product, customer motion, and technical wedge together with Applied Research, Product, Engineering, and the customer. We are selling something much more complex and much more valuable than GPUs: the ability for customers to build their own lab â environments, evals, verifiers, agents, training runs, and deployment loops that compound over time.

You will own that messy middle.

You will work directly with customers, the CEO, GTM leadership, Applied Research, and Engineering to translate ambiguous customer pain into a concrete product strategy, technical scope, commercial proposal, and path to revenue. You will help us figure out where the product is ready, where it needs to be shaped, what the customer actually wants, and how to turn early traction into repeatable motion.

This is a role for someone who wants to be in the room where a new category is being created.


What Youâll Own

Customer-to-Product Translation

You will work with frontier AI labs, fast-growing AI startups, and enterprise AI teams to understand what they are trying to build, where their current stack breaks, and how Prime Intellect can become the infrastructure layer underneath their post-training and agent workflows.

You will turn vague, high-stakes customer conversations into clear technical and commercial strategy:

  • What is the customer actually trying to improve?

  • Is the wedge compute, evals, environments, sandboxes, managed RL, SFT, inference, or a full-stack workflow?

  • What should Applied Research build or prototype?

  • What needs to be packaged as product?

  • What should be in scope for a POC versus a long-term deployment?

  • What is the fastest path to a strong yes?

Pre-PMF Product Strategy

You will help shape Prime Intellectâs product motion before every part of the playbook is obvious.

That means identifying patterns across customer conversations, building repeatable narratives, defining packaging, sharpening use cases, and helping the team understand which customer asks are one-off noise versus signs of a massive market.

You will help answer questions like:

  • How do we explain Lab to different customer segments?

  • Which customer workflows should become reference architectures?

  • What should we productize versus deliver as managed work?

  • Where is the strongest wedge for enterprise customers?

  • Which signals show that a customer is ready for managed post-training?

  • How do we turn Applied Research work into revenue without diluting the research agenda?

Revenue Ownership

You will own high-value customer opportunities from first serious conversation through qualification, scoping, proposal, POC, procurement, and expansion.

You will not be measured on activity. You will be measured on whether the most important customers move.

This includes:

  • Running discovery with technical and executive stakeholders

  • Building the business case and technical wedge

  • Owning account strategy with leadership

  • Drafting proposals, scopes, and commercial structures

  • Coordinating internal workstreams across Applied Research, Product, Engineering, Legal, and Finance

  • Creating momentum through ambiguity

  • Turning early deployments into expansion and long-term platform revenue

Applied Research Partnership

You will work extremely closely with Applied Research.

The best version of this role has enough technical taste to understand where an RL/post-training workflow is real, where a customer is hand-waving, and where a sharp Applied Research prototype could unlock a major deal.

You will help Applied Research prioritize customer-facing work by bringing signal from the field:

  • Which evals matter?

  • Which environments should we build?

  • Which agents or workflows are most commercially valuable?

  • Which technical demos will change the customerâs mind?

  • Which customer problems are actually research problems in disguise?

Category Creation

The market understands compute. It does not yet fully understand full-stack post-training infrastructure.

You will help write the playbook.

You will contribute to positioning, sales narratives, customer decks, case studies, reference architectures, launch moments, and internal strategy. You should be able to turn raw customer conversations into crisp language the entire company can use.


What Weâre Looking For

We are looking for exceptional generalists with rare taste across AI, product, customers, and commercial strategy.

You might come from:

  • Technical GTM at a frontier AI, infra, devtools, or enterprise software company

  • Founder or early operator experience at an AI startup

  • Product or strategy at a highly technical company

  • Forward-deployed engineering, solutions, or applied AI work

  • Investing, venture, or strategic finance with deep AI infrastructure exposure

  • Research-adjacent roles where you worked directly with customers or product teams

You should have:

  • Strong product and commercial judgment

  • Ability to understand technical products quickly

  • Excellent written communication

  • High agency and comfort with ambiguity

  • Taste for what makes a customer problem real

  • Ability to work with researchers, engineers, executives, and operators

  • Sharp instincts around enterprise buying, POCs, procurement, and expansion

  • Obsession with AI, post-training, agents, evals, and infrastructure

  • Ability to create structure where none exists

You do not need to be a researcher, but you should be technical enough to earn trust with researchers and customers.

You do not need to be a traditional salesperson, but you should be commercially intense enough to close.

You do not need to be a PM, but you should have strong product taste.


Bonus Points

  • Experience with RL, SFT, evals, agent frameworks, or LLM post-training

  • Experience selling or deploying infrastructure, AI platforms, devtools, or enterprise AI products

  • Experience working with frontier AI labs, model companies, or infra-heavy startups

  • Ability to write excellent customer-facing decks, memos, proposals, and launch narratives

  • Strong network across AI startups, research labs, or enterprise AI teams

  • Founder mentality and willingness to do unglamorous work to win


Why This Role

Most GTM roles ask you to sell a product someone else already defined.

This role asks you to help define the product, the market, the motion, and the revenue engine at the same time.

You will work on the hardest commercial and product questions at one of the fastest-growing companies in AI infrastructure. You will sit close to customers building real AI systems, researchers pushing the frontier of post-training, and leadership making company-defining decisions.

If you want a clean playbook, this is not the role.

If you want to help invent the playbook for how frontier AI infrastructure gets built, packaged, sold, deployed, and scaled, this is the role.


What We Offer

  • Competitive cash compensation and meaningful equity

  • Flexible work in San Francisco or hybrid-remote

  • Visa sponsorship and relocation support

  • Professional development budget

  • Team off-sites and conference attendance

  • A front-row seat to building the infrastructure layer for open AI


Ready to Build the Commercial Engine for Open Superintelligence?

Apply to help Prime Intellect turn frontier post-training infrastructure into the product, platform, and customer motion that powers the next generation of AI systems.
